svg.title: Perception layer breakdown
svg.desc: The four channels of perception (DOM, Ax-tree, visual, layout) plus the interaction engine that drives state discovery.

g.text: Each node = DOM ref + ax role + bbox + visual crop
"The CTA button" is now one object with all four views attached
From the element graph, identify trigger candidates:
For each: simulate trigger → wait for DOM mutation → re-perceive
Trigger types: click, hover, focus, scroll-to, time-delay, exit-intent
Diff old vs new state → record what content was revealed/changed
Build state graph: initial → S1 (tab2) → S2 (variant Red) → S3 (modal)
Stop conditions: budget cap, no new content, cosmetic-only delta
Element graph + state graph + per-state perception across 4 channels
Heuristic layer can now ask: "is the CTA above the fold in state S0?"
"Does shipping cost only appear after entering address in S2?"
Why all four — none is sufficient alone
DOM has no visual weight · screenshots have no semantics
Ax-tree has no spatial info · layout has no meaning
